sections.title: Short title, extent, application and commencement
sections.content:
(1) This Act may be called the Airports Security Force Act, 1975.
(2) It extends to all aerodromes and airports, all civil aviation stations, centres or offices in Pakistan, all Pakistan air services and activities relating to civil aviation.
(3) It shall apply to all members and officers of the Force wherever they may be.
(4) It shall come into force at once.

metadata.subject_matter: Short title, extent, application and commencement

sections.title: Definitions
sections.content: In this Act, unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context, definitions are provided for terms including 'aerodrome', 'aircraft', 'airport', 'air service', 'air side', 'competent authority', 'Force', 'Director General', 'member', 'officer', 'operator', 'prescribed', 'purposes of civil aviation', 'regulations', and 'rules'.

metadata.subject_matter: Definitions

sections.title: Power to constitute and maintain the Airports Security Force and its functions
sections.content:
(1) The Federal Government may constitute and maintain a force to be called the Airport Security Force for performing the following functions: ensuring security of all airports, aerodromes, aircraft and civil aviation installations; ensuring security of structures and installations; proper conduct of persons at airports; security of passengers, baggage, cargo and mail; maintenance of law and order; and such other functions as required.
(2) Officers and members shall act in accordance with this Act and the rules and regulations.

metadata.subject_matter: Power to constitute and maintain the Airports Security Force and its functions

sections.title: Constitution of the Airports Security Force
sections.content:
(1) The head of the Force shall be a Director General appointed by the Federal Government.
(2) The Force shall consist of such ranks and number of officers and members as prescribed by rules.
(3) Officers and members shall receive prescribed pay, allowances, pensions, leave, and privileges.
(4) On appointment, each officer and member shall receive a certificate vesting them with powers and privileges.
(5) Persons ceasing to be officers or members shall surrender the certificate.
(6) Officers and members shall wear prescribed uniforms.

metadata.subject_matter: Constitution of the Airports Security Force

sections.title: Administration of the Force
sections.content:
(1) The administration of the Force shall vest in the Director General who shall administer it in accordance with the Act, rules, and orders of the Federal Government.
(2) The Director General shall exercise his powers and functions under the supervision and direction of the Federal Government.

metadata.subject_matter: Administration of the Force

sections.title: Short title, extent and commencement
sections.content:
(1) This Act may be called the Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act, 1922.
(2) It extends to the whole of Pakistan.
(3) It shall come into force in any Province or part of a Province on such date as the Provincial Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, direct.

metadata.subject_matter: Short title, extent and commencement

sections.title: Definition
sections.content: In this Act the expression "member of a police-force" means any person appointed or enrolled for the performance of police duties under any enactment specified in the Schedule.

metadata.subject_matter: Definition

sections.title: Penalty for causing disaffection, etc.
sections.content:
Whoever intentionally causes or attempts to cause, or does any act which he knows is likely to cause, disaffection towards Pakistan or the Government established by law in Pakistan amongst the members of a police-force, or induces or attempts to induce, or does any act which he knows is likely to induce, any member of a police-force to withhold his services or to commit a breach of discipline shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to two hundred rupees, or with both.

Explanation: Expressions of disapprobation of the measures of the Government with a view to obtain their alteration by lawful means, or of disapprobation of the administrative or other action of the Government, do not constitute an offence under this section unless they cause or are made for the purpose of causing or are likely to cause disaffection.

metadata.subject_matter: Penalty for causing disaffection, etc.

sections.title: Saving of acts done by police associations and other persons for certain purposes
sections.content:
Nothing shall be deemed to be an offence under this Act which is done in good faith—
(a) for the purpose of promoting the welfare or interest of any member of a police-force by inducing him to withhold his services in any manner, authorised, by law; or
(b) by or on behalf of any association formed for the purpose of furthering the interests of members of a police-force as such, where the association has been authorised or recognised by the Government and the act done is done under any rules or articles of the association which have been approved by the Government.

metadata.subject_matter: Saving of acts done by police associations and other persons for certain purposes

sections.title: Sanction to trial of offences by subordinate Courts
sections.content: No Court shall proceed to the trial of any offence under this Act except with the previous sanction, or on the complaint, of the District Magistrate.

metadata.subject_matter: Sanction to trial of offences by subordinate Courts

sections.title: Trial of cases
sections.content:
(1) No Court inferior to that of a Magistrate of the first class shall try any offence under this Act.
(2) Notwithstanding anything contained in Chapter XXII of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 (V of 1898), no offence under this Act shall be triable summarily.

metadata.subject_matter: Trial of cases

sections.title: Schedule - Enactments defining police-force
sections.content:
The Schedule includes the following enactments for defining members of police-force:
- The Police Act, 1861 (V of 1861)
- The Police Act, 1888 (III of 1888)
- The Bengal Military Police Act, 1892 (V of 1892)
- The Bombay District Police Act, 1890 (IV of 1890)
- The Eastern Frontier Rifles (Bengal Battalion) Act, 1920 (II of 1920)

metadata.subject_matter: Schedule - Enactments defining police-force

sections.title: Short title, extent, application and commencement
sections.content:
(1) This Act may be called the Pakistan Railways Police Act, 1977.
(2) It extends to the whole of Pakistan.
(3) It shall apply to all members and officers of the Pakistan Railways Police wherever they may be.
(4) It shall come into force at once.

metadata.subject_matter: Short title, extent, application and commencement

sections.title: Definitions
sections.content: In this Act, unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context, definitions are provided for terms including 'Chief Executive', 'Code', 'competent authority', 'Inspector-General', 'member', 'offence', 'officer', 'Officer-in-Charge of Railway Police Station', 'Provincial Police', 'Railway', 'railway employee', 'regulations', and 'rules'.

metadata.subject_matter: Definitions

sections.title: Power to constitute, control and maintain the Pakistan Railways Police and its functions
sections.content:
(1) The Federal Government may constitute, control and maintain a force to be called the Pakistan Railways Police for performing functions within specified territorial limits: preventing loss to Railway property, ensuring proper conduct of persons on Railways, maintaining law and order, assisting passengers and railway employees, protecting travellers, keeping platforms clear of idlers, searching empty carriages, regulating vehicle movement, reporting violations, and other functions as required.
(2) The Federal Government may exercise all powers exercisable by a Provincial Government under the Police Act, 1861 and the Code in relation to the Provincial Police.

metadata.subject_matter: Power to constitute, control and maintain the Pakistan Railways Police and its functions

sections.title: Constitution superintendence and administration of the Pakistan Railways Police
sections.content:
(1) The Pakistan Railways Police shall be constituted in such manner as may be prescribed by rules.
(2) The head of the Pakistan Railways Police shall be an Inspector-General who shall administer it in accordance with this Act, rules, regulations, and Federal Government orders.
(3) The Inspector-General shall exercise powers and functions under the general supervision of the Chief Executive.

metadata.subject_matter: Constitution superintendence and administration of the Pakistan Railways Police

sections.title: Transfer of Provincial Railway Police and Railway Watch and Ward to the Pakistan Railways Police
sections.content: All persons holding any rank or post, not above Superintendent, in the Provincial Railway Police and all persons in Railway Watch and Ward are transferred to corresponding posts in the Pakistan Railways Police subject to Federal Government conditions. The Federal Government may revert persons back to Provincial Police if their services are no longer needed.

metadata.subject_matter: Transfer of Provincial Railway Police and Railway Watch and Ward to the Pakistan Railways Police

sections.title: Title and extent
sections.content:
(1) This Act may be called the Police Act, 1888.
(2) It extends to the whole of Pakistan.

metadata.subject_matter: Title and extent

sections.title: Constitution of police forces for special purposes
sections.content:
(1) The Federal Government may, by notification, create a special police district embracing parts of two or more Provinces and extend powers and jurisdiction of police forces to such district.
(2) Members of such police force shall have within every part of any Province included in the district the same powers, duties, privileges and liabilities as in their own Province.
(3) Any member empowered by the Federal Government may exercise powers of officer in charge of a police station in such areas.
(4) A part of a Province included in such district shall not cease to be part of that Province for purposes of police laws.

metadata.subject_matter: Constitution of police forces for special purposes

sections.title: Employment of police officers beyond the province to which they belong
sections.content: A member of the police force of any Province may discharge the functions of a police officer in any part of Pakistan beyond his Province and shall, while doing so, be deemed to be a member of the police force of that part, vested with the same powers, functions, privileges, and liabilities.

metadata.subject_matter: Employment of police officers beyond the province to which they belong

sections.title: Consent of Provincial Government to exercise of powers and jurisdiction
sections.content: Nothing in this Act enables police of one Province to exercise powers and jurisdiction in another Province, not being a railway area, without the consent of that Provincial Government.

metadata.subject_matter: Consent of Provincial Government to exercise of powers and jurisdiction
